entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Selected germplasm lines used in the study

entities.name: SEED TESTA COLOUR
entities.description: The color of the seed coat of groundnut lines

entities.name: WATER UPTAKE RATE
entities.description: The rate at which seeds absorb water, also known as imbibition rate

entities.name: SEED DAMAGE
entities.description: Physical or physiological damage to the seeds

entities.name: SEED LEACHATES
entities.description: Substances that leach out of the seeds during water uptake

entities.name: IN VITRO SEED COLONIZATION
entities.description: The process of fungal colonization of seeds in a controlled laboratory environment

entities.name: SEED ROTS AND AFLAROOT DISEASES
entities.description: Diseases affecting seeds and roots in vivo

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS FLAVUS
entities.description: A fungus that causes seed colonization and seed rots/aflaroot diseases

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: The physical size of the groundnut seeds

relationships.description: No evident relationship between seed coat colour and water uptake rates
Slow water uptake is related to increased seed damage
Slow water uptake is related to characteristics such as seed size
White-seeded lines are highly susceptible to in vitro seed colonization
No correlation between seed coat colour and seed rots and aflaroot diseases
Aspergillus flavus is the organism responsible for in vitro seed colonization
Aspergillus flavus causes seed rots and aflaroot diseases
Groundnut germplasm lines possess different seed testa colours
